# Toobit.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the Toobit REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports Spot and USDT futures trading. Part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem providing shared abstractions across cryptocurrency exchanges.

Toobit.Net provides typed REST clients, WebSocket subscriptions with automatic connection management, typed request/response models, HMAC credentials through `ToobitCredentials`, dependency injection helpers, native AOT support, and CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis support. Targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.

Direct and SharedApis REST methods return `HttpResult<T>` or `HttpResult`; direct and SharedApis WebSocket subscriptions return `WebSocketResult<UpdateSubscription>`. Shared symbol/cache helper methods can return `ExchangeCallResult<T>`. Use `SharedClient.Discover()` to inspect implemented shared interfaces and endpoint/subscription options at runtime. Shared Spot and futures symbol clients expose catalogs after symbol retrieval, support asset type filters, and return inactive-symbol state, display names, and base/quote asset metadata.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/blob/main/README.md): Overview, installation, supported features, basic usage examples, release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/Toobit.Net/): Full documentation hub including client reference, WebSocket subscriptions, dependency injection, and shared interfaces
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/blob/main/docs/ai-api-map.md): Quick intent-to-method map for routing common user requests to the correct client member
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/blob/main/llms-full.txt): Detailed AI/LLM guide with code patterns, endpoint map, common mistakes, and anti-hallucination rules

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/tree/main/Examples/ai-friendly): Compact, fully runnable examples optimized for AI assistants and quick onboarding
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Client setup, ticker, account balances, limit order placement, order status check
- [USDT Futures](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs): Leverage, limit order, positions, close order pattern
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Ticker subscription, kline subscription, user data stream with proper teardown
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): Using CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis for exchange-agnostic code
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): `HttpResult` patterns, retry logic, common errors and their handling
- [Full Examples Repository](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/tree/main/Examples): Console, API, order book, and tracker examples

## Reference

- [CryptoExchange.Net base library](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net): Base abstractions used by Toobit.Net and other exchange libraries
- [CryptoClients.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoClients.Net): Bundle package providing unified access to all exchange libraries
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Toobit.Net): Latest stable release on NuGet

- [Changelog](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/blob/main/Toobit.Net/CHANGELOG.md): Version history

- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/JKorf/Toobit.Net/issues): Bug reports and feature requests
- [GitHub Sponsors](https://github.com/sponsors/JKorf): Support the maintainer
